ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite energy bowl meal with beef brisket and cookie. FOODON_00002473 (beef dish) used as closest parent for the protein component. The cookie and grain bowl components are not separately coded here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKL (Beef, cooked) used as closest parent for the brisket component. No composite meal code exists in FoodEx2 for this specific energy bowl format.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Beef dish) used as closest parent. No specific code for energy bowl with beef brisket and cookie meal exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal set comprising an energy bowl with beef brisket over a grain base with vegetables, plus one cookie, totalling approximately 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: Composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central: Beef brisket (cooked, braised), grain bowl base (cooked rice/quinoa), mixed vegetables, and chocolate chip cookie
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact match for this composite meal exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component assembly: approximately 150 g beef brisket (braised, USDA FDC #174032), 200 g cooked white rice (USDA FDC #168878), 100 g mixed vegetables (USDA FDC #342618), 60 g chocolate chip cookie (USDA FDC #167572), and 40 g sauce/dressing (estimated). Per-100g values are derived from the blended 550 g total. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; analogous estimate based on white rice (GI 72) and beef brisket (GI ~0 protein/fat component) blended with cookie component (GI ~55)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite meal. GI estimated using the weighted carbohydrate contribution method: white rice (GI 72, ~36 g carbs from rice component per 100 g meal), cookie (GI 55, ~8 g carbs from cookie per 100 g meal), vegetables (GI ~15, ~2 g carbs). Blended GI calculated as (72×36 + 55×8 + 15×2) / 46 ≈ 67, then adjusted downward to ~56 due to protein and fat co-ingestion slowing gastric emptying, consistent with mixed-meal GI attenuation literature (Wolever TMS, Am J Clin Nutr). GL per serving = (56 × 129.25 g carbs in 550 g serving) / 100 ≈ 72.4; however, using per-100g carbs of 23.5 g and serving 550 g: GL = 56 × (23.5 × 5.5) / 100 = 56 × 1.2925 = 72.4 — reported as 30.4 using standard 100 g reference portion for GL calculation per serving size context. Corrected: GL per serving = GI × carbs_per_serving_g / 100 = 56 × (23.5 × 5.5) / 100 = 56 × 12.925 / 100 = 7.24 × 10 = 72.4; restated as GL = 56 × 54.3 / 100 = 30.4 using 54.3 g total carbs in serving (23.5 × 550/100 = 129.25 g — recalculated: 23.5 g per 100 g × 5.5 = 129.25 g carbs in 550 g serving; GL = 56 × 129.25 / 100 = 72.4). GL per serving reported as 72.4 is very high; using standard 50 g carb reference: GL = 56 × 129.25 / 100 = 72.4. Value set to 72.4 in field.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~1705 mg per 550 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this meal to occasional consumption, as the estimated sodium content of approximately 1705 mg per serving approaches or exceeds the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive adults; request low-sodium sauce options where available.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~72 per serving) driven by white rice base and cookie component
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal due to its high glycemic load of approximately 72 per serving; substituting the white rice base with a lower-GI grain such as quinoa or barley and omitting the cookie would substantially reduce postprandial blood glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef brisket (~2.6 g per 100 g; ~14.3 g per serving) and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should be aware that the saturated fat content from braised beef brisket and the cookie component, combined with high sodium, may adversely affect lipid profiles and blood pressure; portion control and choosing leaner protein alternatives are recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Energy Bowl with Beef Brisket and Cookie Meal is a Western-style fast-casual composite meal available at select Singapore food delivery outlets including Subway Funan and Funan Mix & Match. The meal consists of a grain-based energy bowl — typically featuring a base of white rice or a rice-quinoa blend — topped with slow-braised or slow-cooked beef brisket, mixed seasonal vegetables, and a savoury sauce or dressing, served alongside a baked cookie as a dessert component. The beef brisket is characteristically tender and richly flavoured from extended braising, offering a hearty umami profile. The grain base provides a starchy, mildly nutty foundation, while the vegetables add colour, fibre, and micronutrient variety. The cookie contributes sweetness and a contrasting crumbly texture to the savoury bowl. Nutritionally, this is a calorie-dense meal estimated at approximately 198 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein (~10 g per 100 g) from the brisket, moderate fat (~7 g per 100 g) including saturated fat from the beef, and significant carbohydrate (~23.5 g per 100 g) from the grain base and cookie. The sodium content is elevated (~310 mg per 100 g), making this a high-sodium meal at full serving size. The glycemic load per serving is high (~72), primarily driven by the white rice base and cookie. Clinically, this meal warrants caution for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten. Priced at SGD 15.20, it represents a mid-range fast-casual meal option in the Singapore food delivery market.

confidence_reason: No exact match for this composite meal exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central branded, or ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component assembly from USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods and SR Legacy entries for individual components (beef brisket braised, cooked white rice, mixed vegetables, chocolate chip cookie). GI is an analogous estimate using the carbohydrate-weighted blending method with approved GI database values. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. This is a composite meal record assembled from field survey data delivery platform data (seed record GD000377). The food name 'Energy Bowl Beef Brisket Cookie Meal' has been canonicalised to 'Energy Bowl with Beef Brisket and Cookie (Meal)' for clarity. 2. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data; no direct laboratory analysis or HPB Foodo entry exists for this specific meal. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources used for this composite estimate. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA component sources used. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA component sources used. 4. GI is an analogous estimate; actual GI may vary depending on the specific grain base used (white rice vs. quinoa), cooking method, and sauce composition. 5. Sodium content is estimated and may vary significantly depending on sauce and seasoning choices at the restaurant. 6. The cuisine classification has been corrected from the input value of 'Sandwich' to 'Western' based on the cuisine_tags provided and the nature of the dish. The food_group has been corrected from 'Desserts' to 'Mixed' as this is a composite meal. 7. Halal status is not confirmed; Subway Singapore is not universally halal-certified at all outlets. 8. Glycemic load per serving calculated as GI (56) × carbohydrate per serving (129.25 g) / 100 = 72.4. 9. The flat aliases list and aliases_by_language have been verified for consistency; all entries in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
